content_en:
The taharah chapter in Nihayat al-Muhtaj is a definitive statement of Shafi'i purification law as understood by one of the school's highest authorities. Ar-Ramli follows the Minhaj closely but adds his own analysis and authoritative determinations, making the chapter particularly valuable for scholars seeking to know what the mu'tamad (relied-upon) position of the later Shafi'i school is on disputed questions.

Ar-Ramli's treatment of the two-qullah threshold for water demonstrates his careful weighing of the evidence. He confirms the Shafi'i position that a small amount of water (less than two qullahs) becomes impure upon any contact with impurity, regardless of any change in the water's characteristics. He acknowledges the hadith of the two qullahs reported in Abu Dawud and notes the disagreement among hadith scholars about its exact chain, but upholds the Shafi'i school's reliance on it as the basis for its water classification system.

On the question of musta'mal water (water that has dripped from the limbs during an obligatory purification), ar-Ramli confirms the Shafi'i position that it is tahir (pure) in itself but not mutahhir (purifying) — it cannot be used for a second obligatory purification. He addresses edge cases: if the water that drips from a limb falls back into a vessel before completing the required washing, does that water in the vessel become musta'mal? His analysis distinguishes between water that has done its 'legal work' (completed the purification of that limb) and water that is still in the process of purifying.

Ar-Ramli gives careful attention to the Shafi'i school's classification of impurities into three categories. He confirms that the impurity of dogs and pigs is mughallazah (heavy) requiring sevenfold washing with earth (or its substitute, such as soap or ash). He examines the question of whether the pig has its own textual basis for heavy impurity or whether its heavy impurity derives by analogy from the dog — a scholarly discussion where he confirms the Shafi'i position that both animals share the mughallazah status.

For wudu, ar-Ramli restates and amplifies the Shafi'i requirement of niyyah (intention) as the first obligatory element, explaining that the intention must be present in the heart and that the verbal statement of intention (though recommended by some) is not required. He addresses the timing of the intention with precision: it must accompany the first washing of the face (the first fard act), not merely precede it by a long interval without continuity.

Ar-Ramli's treatment of the invalidators of wudu includes an important discussion of the ruling on touching a member of the opposite sex. The Shafi'i school holds that touching a non-mahram woman (or man, in the case of a woman) breaks wudu — based on the Quranic phrase 'or you have touched women' (al-Ma'idah 5:6), which the Shafi'i school interprets as referring to touching, not sexual intercourse (the interpretation of the Hanafi school). Ar-Ramli explains this interpretation and notes that the touch must be skin-to-skin without any barrier, and that the touched person's wudu is not broken — only the person who initiates the touch.

The chapter on ghusl and tayammum follows the same pattern: ar-Ramli states the established Shafi'i positions, indicates what is asahh (most correct) when there is internal disagreement, and supplies his own occasional determinations that settle disputes. His authority in these matters derives from his mastery of the full range of Shafi'i sources and his standing as one of the school's most trusted later authorities.
content_id:
Bab taharah dalam Nihayat al-Muhtaj adalah pernyataan definitif tentang hukum bersuci Syafi'i sebagaimana dipahami oleh salah satu otoritas tertinggi mazhab. Ar-Ramli mengikuti Minhaj secara seksama namun menambahkan analisis dan penetapan otoritatifnya sendiri, sehingga bab ini sangat berharga bagi ulama yang ingin mengetahui posisi mu'tamad (yang diandalkan) mazhab Syafi'i akhir pada permasalahan yang diperdebatkan.

Pembahasan ar-Ramli tentang ambang batas dua qullah untuk air menunjukkan pertimbangannya yang cermat atas bukti-bukti yang ada. Beliau mengonfirmasi posisi Syafi'i bahwa air dalam jumlah kecil (kurang dari dua qullah) menjadi najis ketika bersentuhan dengan najis apa pun, tanpa memandang perubahan pada karakteristik air. Beliau mengakui hadis dua qullah yang diriwayatkan dalam Abu Dawud dan mencatat adanya perbedaan pendapat di antara ulama hadis tentang sanadnya, namun tetap mempertahankan ketergantungan mazhab Syafi'i padanya sebagai dasar sistem klasifikasi airnya.

Tentang air musta'mal (air yang menetes dari anggota badan selama bersuci wajib), ar-Ramli mengonfirmasi posisi Syafi'i bahwa air tersebut adalah tahir (suci) pada dirinya sendiri tetapi tidak mutahhir (menyucikan) — air itu tidak dapat digunakan untuk bersuci wajib yang kedua. Beliau membahas kasus-kasus tepi: jika air yang menetes dari anggota badan jatuh kembali ke dalam wadah sebelum pencucian yang diperlukan selesai, apakah air dalam wadah itu menjadi musta'mal? Analisisnya membedakan antara air yang telah melakukan 'pekerjaan hukumnya' (menyelesaikan penyucian anggota badan tersebut) dan air yang masih dalam proses penyucian.

Ar-Ramli memberikan perhatian cermat terhadap klasifikasi najis mazhab Syafi'i ke dalam tiga kategori. Beliau mengonfirmasi bahwa najis anjing dan babi adalah mughallazah (berat) yang memerlukan pencucian tujuh kali dengan salah satunya menggunakan tanah untuk pensucian yang valid.
content_ar: هذا الفصل من الكتاب يتناول موضوعات إسلامية مهمة في إطار منهج أهل السنة والجماعة.
